summary: for multi-function teaching, user wants per-function code+explain alternation instead of bulk code then bulk explanation
trigger: AI showed all six output-layer function bodies in one block then explained them grouped under one heading
directive: when teaching N functions, alternate: show function 1 code -> explain function 1 -> show function 2 -> explain function 2 -> ... so each explanation lands while its code is still in the reader's eye

summary: user noticed AI skipped the no-vibe bookkeeping: no Turn Response Contract header, no ai-notes logging, no session JSON across multiple turns
trigger: AI taught two layers (resize fix, VT escapes) without emitting the [no-vibe] header, without logging the prior preference as an ai-note, and without creating .no-vibe/data/sessions/<slug>.json or .no-vibe/session.md
directive: follow data-logging.md per-turn action order strictly on every reply while no-vibe is ON: read session JSON, run pre-turn audit when errors_this_session>=1, emit the [no-vibe] header as the first line, act, log triggering events to ai-notes/mistakes, update session JSON if any tracked field changed

summary: Layer 4 (front/back diff in lt_present) introduced a visible regression: prior shell scrollback shows through unrendered cells because lt__buffer_resize initializes back and front identically, making the first lt_present a no-op
trigger: user ran hello.exe after Layer 5 and saw 'old terminal history still there' alongside the rendered message
directive: before introducing diff-based optimizations, audit ALL initialization paths that touch the buffers being diffed: if both buffers start equal, the diff's first-frame postcondition is broken. Pattern generalizes — any cache-invalidation predicate must hold on init, not just on transitions during steady state

summary: lt_init must NOT clear the screen. lt_clear is the SOLE opt-in for blanket background fill. Cells with ch=0 mean 'transparent / unset' and must not emit anything to the terminal — libterm draws on top of whatever the terminal already shows.
trigger: user observed that after Layer 6's front-sentinel fix, hello.exe was clearing the terminal even without an lt_clear() call, because lt__buffer_resize fills back with (' ', clear_fg, clear_bg) and the diff renders that as a screen-wide space-paint
directive: treat zero-cells as semantically transparent. lt__buffer_resize leaves back as zeros (not space-filled). lt_present's diff branch must skip emission for cells where bc->ch == 0 (sync front so future frames are honest, but emit no bytes and don't move the cursor cache). Document that turning a previously-rendered cell back to transparent requires a full lt_clear-and-redraw cycle
